stories.headline: How Will Trump-Xi Summit Impact US Policy on Taiwan?
stories.summary: Shirley Syaru Lin, founder and chair of the Center for Asia-Pacific Resilience and Innovation, says it would be a mistake for the US to treat Taiwan as a bargaining chip because of its strategic and economic importance. Speaking to Bloomberg's The China Show, she also warns that Washington’s unpredictability on Taiwan policy risks creating more uncertainty. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China offers US to help open Strait of Hormuz, but warns Trump over Taiwan
stories.summary: Trump is set to conclude his two-day state visit in China with lunch and tea with Xi, as the US leader looks to shift attention toward trade talks that have so far produced little breakthrough.

stories.headline: CIA chief visits Cuba as energy crisis worsens
stories.summary: The reported visit to Havana came after the US renewed an offer of aid to ease the effects of its oil blockade.

stories.headline: Boeing shares slide as Trump’s China summit deals disappoint
stories.summary: US president says Beijing will expand agriculture and oil purchases

stories.headline: How Trump-Xi Summit Could Reshape US-China Trade
stories.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer says he anticipates that China would commit to billions in American agricultural purchases, as Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping complete their summit in Beijing. Hogan Lovells internation trade lawyer Ben Kostrzewa discusses how the summit could reshape the two countries' trade relationship. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: C.I.A. Director Visits Cuba as Tensions Rise and Island Runs Out of Oil
stories.summary: John Ratcliffe, the C.I.A. director, is the highest-ranking official in the Trump administration to visit the country.

stories.headline: Eurovision braces for new protests over Israel’s participation
stories.summary: Eurovision is heading towards its grand final, but organisers are bracing for more demonstrations against Israel.

stories.headline: Trump and Xi kick off talks in crucial summit
stories.summary: Chinese state media hail a new era of ‘strategic stability’ as leaders discuss trade and geopolitics

stories.headline: Former USTR Tai Says US-China Trade Ties at Crossroads
stories.summary: Former US Trade Representative Katherine Tai unpacks the significance of the summit between Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump. She also outlines how the meeting could reshape US-China trade relations, highlighting both opportunities for cooperation and looming challenges. She speaks with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: India Hikes Fuel Prices For The First Time in Four Years
stories.summary: India’s state-run refiners have raised fuel prices for the first time in four years, as New Delhi yields to crude prices pushed higher by the war in Iran, and to the resulting financial pressures battering its processors.

stories.headline: Comparing China and US Statements on Trump-Xi Summit
stories.summary: President Donald Trump's visit to Beijing has dominated Chinese state media, with news stories and commentary mostly striking a positive tone. Despite the grand ceremonies and glowing coverage, however, a comparison of summit readouts from the US and China suggests differences remain on key issues. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Malaysia Revises Up GDP Growth, Says Key Rate Appropriate
stories.summary: Malaysia’s economy grew faster than initially estimated in the first quarter despite a sharp slowdown in March, supported by resilient household spending and the artificial-intelligence boom.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Euro Dream Is Reshaping Bond Markets in Eastern Europe
stories.summary: Hungary’s ambition to join the euro is changing the hierarchy of eastern European bond markets in a way that hasn’t been seen in years.

stories.headline: Adtek Files for Hong Kong IPO, Adding to Chinese AI Listings
stories.summary: Shenzhen Adtek Technology Co. has filed for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, adding to a wave of listings by Chinese companies related to data centers and artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: Chinese Steel Mill Profitability at Highest Level Since August
stories.summary: China’s steel mill profitability has climbed to the highest since August, according to data from consultancy Mysteel, although questions remain over how long the improvement can last.

stories.headline: Latvia prime minister resigns days after ‘stray’ drone incursion – as it happened

stories.headline: Canada is welcome to join Eurovision, says song contest director
stories.summary: ‘We know that Mark Carney wants to sort of embrace Europe,’ says competition director Martin GreenCanada is welcome to join Eurovision if it wishes, its director has said, months after the country revealed it wanted to “explore” joining the song contest in its federal budget.Eurovision director Martin Green told the BBC on Wednesday that Canada hadn’t yet applied, but would be welcome to. stories.headline: Iran, Taiwan, and trade: Trump’s high‑stakes return to Beijing

stories.headline: Ex-defence minister admits Latvia faces security 'questions' but no silver bullet to stop drones

stories.headline: "Super Mario" celebrated for defending the euro and European unity
stories.summary: Former European Central Bank president Mario Draghi received the 2026 Charlemagne Prize on Thursday, 14 May, in Aachen, Germany, in recognition of his contributions to European unity and his leadership during the eurozone debt crisis of the 2010s.

stories.headline: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption verdict

stories.summary: In recent decades, the federal authorities have generally revoked U.S. citizenship from people accused of wrongdoing on their citizenship applications. A new case focuses on a crime committed later.

stories.headline: Rubio is banned from China, but Beijing may have found a way around it

stories.summary: Under pressure from Beijing, the president has indicated an openness to rethinking U.S. support for Taiwan, alarming its backers.

stories.summary: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.
